extraction.one_line_summary: WILLIAMS Stephen won uphill sprint after 7-rider break caught inside final km, dropping pure sprinters KRISTOFF and SAGAN on circuit climbs
tactical_patterns: SIMMONS Quinn and PELLAUD Simon bridged break then battled KOM points, wasting energy before caught final 3km
OLIVEIRA Nelson and FUGLSANG Jakob attacked together approaching finish before group recaught, revealing coordination testing peloton
VAN WILDER Ilan led out sprint despite teammates away earlier, suggesting Soudal committed to stage not break strategy

rider_signals.WILLIAMS Stephen: positive: First WorldTour win outsprinting GC contenders on punchy finish suggests punch-climbing form exceeds expectation
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: positive: Second place to climbing-specialist shows punchy form despite Tour de France GC focus
rider_signals.KRON Andreas: positive: Third place and best young rider suggests unexpected stage race form for typically classics-focused rider
rider_signals.HIRSCHI Marc: neutral: Fourth as home favorite shows solid but not dominant form before Tour de France
rider_signals.LUTSENKO Alexey: positive: Fifth place in reduced sprint indicates climbing legs sharper than past stage race arrivals
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: negative: Dropped on final circuit climbs in opening stage signals concerning form deficit before Tour preparation concludes
rider_signals.MARTÍNEZ Daniel Felipe: negative: Going backwards on finale suggests either illness or fitness deficit unexpected for GC contender
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: neutral: Dropped on circuit climbs confirms pure sprinter categorization, no residual climbing from classics season
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Eliminated on circuit confirms reduced climbing tolerance post-prime years
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: Active in finale attacks with EVENEPOEL suggests aggressive GC racing approach beyond expected conservative tempo
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Animating finale with VLASOV despite Stage 1 reveals willingness to test legs early rather than conserve
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Paced group back to attackers showing diesel strength and team leadership despite not contesting sprint
rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: Present in front group but not contesting sprint suggests controlled recon ride before Tour
rider_signals.HIGUITA Sergio: positive: Top-10 finish signals climbing sharpness maintained after Giro d'Italia effort
rider_signals.POZZOVIVO Domenico: positive: Top-10 at age 39 on punchy finish suggests maintained specific power despite Grand Tour proximity
rider_signals.VINE Jay: negative: Dropped from quality break on penultimate climb reveals climbing endurance gap versus one-day punch
rider_signals.ASKEY Lewis: negative: First dropped from break on circuit climbs confirms development rider status among WorldTour quality
rider_signals.HAGA Chad: neutral: Survived longer in quality break showing residual strength but ultimately caught shows ceiling
